reasoning: Between April and May 2025, France actively spearheaded an initiative to officially recognize a Palestinian state alongside the UK and Canada. This diplomatic push is explicitly targeted to culminate at a UN conference co-hosted by France and Saudi Arabia scheduled for June 2025 (likely June 17-20). While some mid-May reports featured UK officials suspecting Macron might delay the announcement, subsequent statements on May 20 confirmed a strong trilateral commitment to prepare the recognition. A May 28 update indicated French officials are still 'weighing up the move' ahead of the conference, injecting some uncertainty regarding whether formal execution will happen in June or be delayed. However, the multi-national coordination, high-level public commitments (including from PM Bayrou and FM Lammy), and the specific targeting of a June UN conference event provide strong evidence that at least one of these countries will formalize their recognition in June 2025. Given the typical risks of delay in high-stakes Middle East diplomacy, the probability is estimated at a moderately high 71%.
